source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane / O-Ring Failure

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - O-Ring Failure on Aceco Compact Valve
actions_taken: Control Room Operator initiated facility Emergency Shut Down
Telemark well secured by closing SCSSV, PMV, PWV and FLIV
All personnel mustered and accounted for
Emergency response team activated and utilized Fire Water hose reels to disperse methanol
Riser pressure bled down to 2,000 psig before manual boarding valve closed
Leak and spill reported to Witt O'Brien, NRC, and BSEE
event.summary: On 16-FEB-2015 at 12:40 hours, the Titan Platform (OCS-G 16661) experienced an O-Ring failure on a 1" 15,000 psig Aceco compact valve on the Telemark (AT 63 No. 4) incoming flowline. The valve was being used during methanol flow-back operations following hydrate remediation. Approximately 438 barrels of methanol with trace amounts of crude oil leaked into the Gulf of Mexico, creating a barely-visible sheen over a 1,320' by 10' area. Root causes included improper installation by a Shamrock technician: flange cap screws were not properly torqued, an incorrect outlet flange was installed, and the face seal was not properly verified. No injuries or fatalities occurred.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District made no recommendations to the Agency

hazards.name: High-pressure hydrocarbon and methanol in flowline system
Pressurized valve assembly with sealing components

threats.name: Improper installation and torquing of valve flange bolts
Installation of incorrect outlet flange incompatible with valve seal
Failure to verify proper positioning of face seals during installation
Technician lack of knowledge regarding incompatible flange installation

consequences.name: Loss of containment - methanol and crude oil discharge to Gulf of Mexico
Environmental pollution - visible sheen covering 1,320' by 10' area
Potential personnel exposure to methanol vapors and high-pressure release

controls.name: Manufacturer Installation, Operations & Maintenance Procedures for Aceco Valve
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure correct valve installation
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent improper installation

supporting_text: the technician did not verify the correct positioning of the face seals and the end flange bolts were not torqued properly according to the Operations & Maintenance Manual
Technician did not follow the (ACECO VALVE) Installation, Operations & Maintenance procedures when installing the valve in the system

controls.name: Torque wrench requirement for flange bolt tightening
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure proper bolt tension
controls.lod_basis: Manufacturer-specified tool to achieve correct torque values

supporting_text: Tech did not adhere to the manufacturer's recommendation to utilize a torque wrench for tightening the bolts
the #1 cap screw on the outlet flange (top) was significantly over-torqued at 210+ ft-lbs
After torque values were recorded on all cap screws... the valve was found to have been both over & under torqued during the installation

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for valve installation work
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to plan and communicate safe work procedures
controls.lod_basis: Pre-job planning to identify hazards and control measures

supporting_text: Lessee Did Not Mention in the Job Safety Analysis (JSA) of the Tighten of flange bolts using the bolt torquing methods of the Aceco 15K psig valve dated 2-10-2015
Lessee Did Not Follow the Factory Installation, Operations, and Maintenance procedures in the Job Safety Analysis JSA dated 2-10-2015

controls.name: Technician competence and knowledge verification for valve repair
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure qualified personnel perform critical work
controls.lod_basis: Personnel qualification to prevent human error in technical tasks

supporting_text: The third party technician failed to identify that the wrong flange was in place on the outlet end of the valve
Lessee did not ensure all parties involved in the job were knowledgeable and experienced for the repair in recognizing incompatible Flange installed

controls.name: Control Room Operator detection and emergency shutdown system
controls.barrier_role: Detect loss of containment and initiate emergency response
controls.lod_basis: Automated and manual detection of abnormal conditions to trigger shutdown

supporting_text: Immediately upon detecting the failure of the O-ring, the Control Room Operator initiated a facility Emergency Shut Down and sounded the general alarm
The Telemark well was secured by closing the SCSSV, PMV, PWV and FLIV

controls.name: Emergency response team and fire water hose reels
controls.barrier_role: Contain and disperse leaked methanol to minimize environmental impact
controls.lod_basis: Active response to mitigate consequences of loss of containment

supporting_text: The Titan's emergency response team was activated and utilized Fire Water hose reels to disperse the methanol leaking from the valve
the riser pressure was bled down to 2,000 psig +/- before the emergency response team moved in and closed the manual boarding valve to stop the leak

controls.name: Personnel muster and accountability system
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all personnel are accounted for and safe during emergency
controls.lod_basis: Safety protocol to verify no personnel are at risk

supporting_text: All personnel mustered and were accounted for and it was determined there were no injuries or fatalities

controls.name: Incident reporting to regulatory authorities (NRC and BSEE)
controls.barrier_role: Ensure regulatory notification and investigation of incident
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident reporting and investigation to prevent recurrence

supporting_text: Facility personnel reported the leak and spill to Witt O'Brien, who in turn, made the appropriate notification to NRC (No. 1108280) and BSEE

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
